text.title: Nginx Configuration Hardening Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules grounding the nginx-configuration methodology, each paired with rationale and source reference.

rule.statement: Configure TLS 1.2 + TLS 1.3 only, with ssl_ciphers from a current Mozilla 'modern' or 'intermediate' profile and ssl_prefer_server_ciphers on.
rule.rationale: Older TLS versions and ciphers are documented CVE territory; Mozilla profiles track current best practice.

rule.statement: Every upstream block sets keepalive, keepalive_timeout and an active health check (Plus) or proxy_next_upstream + max_fails fallback.
rule.rationale: Without keepalive nginx burns CPU on TCP handshake per request; without health checks dead upstreams keep receiving traffic.

rule.statement: Define per-route rate-limit zones (limit_req_zone) sized to expected traffic + 2× burst, applied at minimum to /login, /signup and write endpoints.
rule.rationale: Unbounded request rate is the recurring DoS root cause for nginx-fronted services.

rule.statement: Emit baseline security headers — Strict-Transport-Security, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options or CSP frame-ancestors, Referrer-Policy — in the server block.
rule.rationale: Browser-side hardening costs nothing once configured; missing headers re-open mitigated attack classes.

rule.statement: Configure proxy_cache_path with explicit max_size and inactive timeout; cache lock to avoid thundering-herd on a cache miss.
rule.rationale: Unsized caches fill the disk; missing cache lock multiplies origin requests on every cache miss.

rule.statement: Set client_max_body_size, large_client_header_buffers and client_header_timeout explicitly per server block.
rule.rationale: Defaults are too generous for public-facing nginx — explicit limits make slow-body DoS unfeasible.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `Applies If` are not all true, do NOT run this methodology — return early with reason.
rule.rationale: Decision tree's negative branch must terminate cleanly without partial output.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold AND scope is explicit, run the methodology end-to-end and produce the canonical artefact.
rule.rationale: Decision tree positive-branch terminus; aliases the apply-end-to-end verdict.
